questions: What are the exact mechanics of the Accountable drift-detection + issue-lifecycle pattern?
Which Qor-logic health checks are automatable tonight, and what gaps block aggregation?
How should #250 scope split to avoid a half-measure closure?

findings.claim: Drift-detection recipe is complete: cron + self-test against ephemeral state + JSON-last-line contract + idempotent gh issue search/comment/create/close
11 CLI-invocable read-only checks with clean exits, but text-only output, no scheduled workflow, no issues:write, no aggregate JSON
The nightly workflow needs a machine-readable aggregate first -- exactly GH #240; build it in the same phase
Phase 165 ships aggregate + nightly workflow + self-test (closes #240, advances #250); dry-run modes for 6 mutating commands go to phase 166 to close #250 fully
